summary: Preserve client_id alongside auth_token in sanitizeCookieJar so paid Skool communities authenticate instead of 307-redirecting to /about.
reason: The original cookie-jar-jwt-sanitize patch (filed for macOS pycookiecheat encrypted_value defense) stripped every cookie except auth_token. Free Skool communities authenticate with auth_token alone, so this looked correct in dogfood; paid communities (verified against ai-automation-society-plus) reject auth_token-only requests with a 307 to /about because Skool's edge requires the client_id cookie too. Verified via curl: full cookie jar returns classroom.json payload, auth_token-only redirects. Fix preserves client_id when its value matches an ASCII allowlist (clientIDPattern) so Chrome encrypted_value bytes still can't leak into config.toml.
validated_outcome: `go test ./internal/cli/...` passes the new sanitize-cookie-jar table tests; `go build ./...` clean; `go vet ./...` clean. Manual curl reproduction in the user's session confirms paid-community classroom.json returns full payload with client_id preserved.
upstream_issue: cli-printing-press: cookie-sanitizer template assumes a single auth cookie; APIs that require a session-pair (auth + client/device id) need a generator-level allowlist hook so every printed CLI doesn't hand-roll this filter.
